events.description: Frodo leaves Bag End. The Ring-bearer sets out from Hobbiton at last, Sam and Pippin at his side.
A Black Rider on the road. The first of the Nazgul is seen sniffing along the lanes of the Shire.
The Hunt for the Ring. Sauron looses all Nine Riders into Eriador to seize the Ring-bearer.
Into the Old Forest. The hobbits are saved by Tom Bombadil.
Captured on the Barrow-downs. A Barrow-wight takes the hobbits before Bombadil frees them.
The Prancing Pony at Bree. The hobbits meet Strider, a Ranger of the North.
Camp under Weathertop. Frodo is stabbed by the Witch-king with a Morgul-knife.
Flight to the Ford of Bruinen. The river rises against the Nine and Frodo passes into Rivendell.
The Council of Elrond. The Fellowship of the Ring is chosen and the Ring's doom is set.
The Eye searches northward. Word of the Halflings and the Shire reaches the depths of Barad-dur.
Saruman's hand at work. Wormtongue's whispers weaken Theoden while Isengard arms openly for war.
The Fellowship leaves Rivendell. Nine walkers set out against the Nine Riders.
The Fellowship reaches Hollin, the old land of Eregion.
Snow on Caradhras. The cruel mountain turns the company back from the high pass.
The West-gate of Moria. Wargs attack in the night; Gollum begins to trail the Ring-bearer.
The Bridge of Khazad-dum. Gandalf falls with the Balrog into shadow.
Caras Galadhon. The company comes to the city of the Galadhrim and meets Galadriel.
Gandalf casts down the Balrog upon the peak of Zirakzigil, and his life departs.
Farewell to Lorien. The Fellowship departs by boat with Galadriel's gifts.
First Battle of the Fords of Isen. Theodred, son of Theoden, is slain by Saruman's Uruks.
The Breaking of the Fellowship. Boromir falls; Frodo and Sam strike east alone.
Saruman's raiders seize Merry and Pippin, hunting the Ring-bearer for the White Hand.
Gandalf returns, robed in white, and finds the Three Hunters in Fangorn.
Second Battle of the Fords of Isen. Rohan's defense is overrun and the road to Helm's Deep lies open.
Battle of the Hornburg at Helm's Deep. The host of Isengard breaks upon the Deeping Wall.
Isengard is drowned. The Ents tear down Saruman's walls and loose the Isen upon his pits.
The Voice of Saruman is broken. Gandalf casts him from the Order; Wormtongue hurls down the palantir.
The Dawnless Day. A shadow flows out of Mordor; Aragorn takes the Paths of the Dead.
The Witch-king leads the host of Minas Morgul forth, and night marches with it out of the east.
Dol Guldur assails Lothlorien. The first assault breaks upon the Golden Wood.
Frodo is taken by Orcs at Cirith Ungol; Sam alone carries the Ring on into Mordor.
The siege of Gondor. Sauron's host crosses the Anduin and rings Minas Tirith with fire.
The Battle of the Pelennor Fields. Theoden falls; the Witch-king is slain by Eowyn and Merry.
War in the North. The hosts of Dol Guldur and Rhun fall upon Lorien, Thranduil's realm, and Dale.
The Host of the West marches from Minas Tirith to the Black Gate as a desperate feint.
Sauron takes the bait. The Eye fixes upon the gates of Mordor, blind to two hobbits in the ash.
The Ring goes into the Fire. Barad-dur falls and the Shadow passes from the world.
The fall of Barad-dur. With the Ring unmade, the Dark Tower crumbles and Sauron is no more.
